mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate over a short period indicates a cyclical downturn is underway, leading to lower expected corporate earnings and negative equity returns as investors price in a recession.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses a similar unemployment-based trigger to identify recessions in real-time.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met during a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals strong central bank support for the economy, lowering the discount rate for future earnings and boosting equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed cut rates from 5.25% in Sep 2007 to 2.25% by Apr 2008, an aggressive easing cycle.
failure_condition: A 3-month change in FEDFUNDS has a statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) or negative correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing real economic activity, proxied by retail sales, and persistent inflation, proxied by CPI, creates a stagflationary environment that erodes corporate profit margins and increases equity risk premia, leading to negative returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth > 3.5% AND year-over-year RSAFS growth < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Headline CPI was running over 4% YoY in early 2008 while retail sales growth was weakening.
failure_condition: An interaction term between CPIAUCSL momentum and RSAFS momentum is not a statistically significant negative predictor of 60-day forward returns in a walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production reflects broad-based weakness in the manufacturing and production sectors. This directly impacts corporate revenues and profitability, leading to negative equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO peaked in late 2007 and was showing clear declines by early 2008.
failure_condition: The 3-month momentum of INDPRO fails to show a rank correlation greater than 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a key leading indicator of residential investment. A collapse below a critical threshold signals a severe contraction in the housing sector with negative spillovers to the financial system and consumer spending, depressing equity prices.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level falls below 1,100 thousands of units.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts peaked above 2,200 in 2006 and were in freefall by April 2008.
failure_condition: In periods where HOUST is below 1,100, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower than in periods where it is above 1,100.

mechanism_narrative: The monthly change in nonfarm payrolls is a high-frequency, direct measure of economic momentum. Negative readings (job losses) signal an ongoing contraction, reducing aggregate income and consumption, which negatively impacts equity returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS showed job losses in Jan, Feb, and Mar 2008, confirming a downturn.
failure_condition: The sign of the 1-month change in PAYEMS does not predict the sign of the 5-day forward S&P 500 return with accuracy statistically greater than 50% (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: While Fed easing is typically bullish, its positive effect is reversed during periods of rapid labor market collapse. In this regime, monetary policy is 'pushing on a string' as credit risk and recession fears overwhelm the discount rate effect, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Despite aggressive Fed cuts from late 2007, equity markets continued to decline as unemployment rose.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is rising sharply, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: The quarter-over-quarter growth rate of real GDP is the broadest measure of economic health. A decelerating or negative growth rate indicates a slowdown in aggregate demand, which translates directly to lower corporate revenue growth and negative pressure on stock prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2007 real GDP growth was revised down to -0.2%, signaling the start of a recession.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly real GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 10-year rolling window.
